title: Eyes On The Prize

jokes.text: Geoffrey's eyebrows just grew back... from the time you tried to fix the stove, man.
jokes.setup: Uncle Phil trying to fix the sink despite past disasters
jokes.punchline: Reference to previous incident where Phil's repair work apparently burned off Geoffrey's eyebrows
jokes.explanation: Strong character-based humor that establishes Phil's incompetence through vivid physical consequence. The eyebrow detail is specific and memorable.

jokes.text: Have you forgotten the toaster incident, sweetie? Geoffrey hasn't. How was I supposed to know a piece of whole wheat would scratch a man's cornea?
jokes.setup: Phil defending his repair abilities
jokes.punchline: Revealing increasingly bizarre consequences of his past repair attempts
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly absurd escalation - how does fixing a toaster lead to bread scratching someone's cornea? The specificity sells the absurdity.

jokes.text: Certainly got the pants for it. -I'm calling the plumber. -I'm calling the tailor.
jokes.setup: Phil claiming he knows plumbing
jokes.punchline: Will mocking Phil's plumber's crack/ill-fitting pants, followed by dual comeback
jokes.explanation: Classic visual gag about plumber's crack with efficient dual punchline structure. Not groundbreaking but well-executed.

jokes.text: There's no dial tone. There's no static, either.
jokes.setup: Phil claiming he fixed the phone static by rewiring
jokes.punchline: Vivian's dry observation that he eliminated the static by destroying the phone entirely
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan delivery. The logic is ironically sound - no static because no phone service at all.

jokes.text: Yeah, right. Right after your Uncle Philip repairs something that doesn't send Geoffrey to the emergency room.
jokes.setup: Will claiming he'll win a Corvette on a game show
jokes.punchline: Sarcastic comparison to Phil's impossible track record of safe repairs
jokes.explanation: Good callback that reinforces the running gag while advancing the plot. Emergency room escalates the stakes.

jokes.text: All right. Aunt Viv, I'll push... and if you spackle real fast, we could probably wall him in.
jokes.setup: Phil presumably stuck behind/in something during his repair attempt
jokes.punchline: Will suggesting they entomb Phil in the wall like a mob hit
jokes.explanation: Darkly funny solution to the Phil problem. The casual, practical tone makes the murderous suggestion funnier.

jokes.text: No phone service for 24 hours? This is a nightmare. I mean, it's like we've vanished off the face of the earth!
jokes.setup: Learning they have no phone service temporarily
jokes.punchline: Hilary's dramatic overreaction treating minor inconvenience as existential crisis
jokes.explanation: Classic Hilary character moment - her privileged perspective makes minor problems seem catastrophic. Good character work.

jokes.text: Hey, she's not being dramatic. Jimmie Walker's phone went out for 24 hours in the late 70s... and, hey, well, y'all know the rest.
jokes.setup: Someone dismissing Hilary's dramatic reaction to phone outage
jokes.punchline: Will implying Jimmie Walker's career decline was due to phone service interruption
jokes.explanation: Clever meta-joke about celebrity career decline. The 'y'all know the rest' sells it by making the audience fill in the tragic implication.

jokes.text: Excuse my language, Dad, but it's got to be said.... I'm behind you, big guy.
jokes.setup: Carlton dramatically apologizing for language he's about to use
jokes.punchline: The 'offensive' language is just mild encouragement
jokes.explanation: Perfect Carlton moment - his uptight nature makes even mild support seem dangerously rebellious to him. Great misdirection.

jokes.text: Sure, honey, in four years. I'm sure the phone will still be broken.
jokes.setup: Ashley asking to drive to use someone else's phone
jokes.punchline: Vivian sarcastically noting Ashley can't drive for years, with dig at Phil's repair timeline
jokes.explanation: Solid two-layer joke - Ashley's too young to drive, and Phil's so bad at repairs it'll take that long anyway.

jokes.text: leather on the seats, and Halle Berry on my lap.
jokes.setup: Will describing his dream Corvette features
jokes.punchline: Escalating from car features to having a celebrity as an accessory
jokes.explanation: Standard teenage male fantasy humor. The escalation works but it's not particularly original or clever.

jokes.text: All I gotta do is answer a couple questions... and let my partner squirt some stuff all over me. Wasn't Jimmy Swaggart just arrested for that?
jokes.setup: Will innocently describing the game show format
jokes.punchline: Carlton connecting it to Jimmy Swaggart's sex scandal
jokes.explanation: Brilliant double entendre that catches the sexual innuendo in innocent game show description. Carlton delivering it makes it even funnier.

jokes.text: The next person that mentions the phone, gets a boot in their behind. Mr. Banks, your phone is broken.
jokes.setup: Phil threatening anyone who mentions the phone
jokes.punchline: Geoffrey immediately mentioning the phone in the most direct way possible
jokes.explanation: Perfect timing and Geoffrey's dry delivery. The immediate contradiction is beautifully executed.

jokes.text: It occurs to me, this family's got a lot to learn about hospitality.
jokes.setup: Phil presumably threatening Geoffrey after the phone comment
jokes.punchline: Geoffrey's understated British criticism of being threatened with violence
jokes.explanation: Classic Geoffrey - his dry, understated response to chaos. The word 'hospitality' is perfectly chosen for the situation.

jokes.text: He's the source that revealed that Mariah Carey's black.
jokes.setup: Jazz explaining how Willy at Roscoe's knows everything
jokes.punchline: Crediting him with 'revealing' Mariah Carey's racial background as if it were secret intel
jokes.explanation: Funny take on how celebrity racial identity was often ambiguous in the media. Treating it like classified information is absurd.

jokes.text: You sold me a fake Rolex. You paid with a fake 20.
jokes.setup: Conflict about a watch transaction
jokes.punchline: Revealing both parties were scamming each other equally
jokes.explanation: Perfect symmetry - both characters are equally culpable. The economy of revealing two scams in one exchange is efficient.

jokes.text: you should have known that it was fake when you saw that the warranty was only for two hours.
jokes.setup: Mediating the fake Rolex dispute
jokes.punchline: Pointing out an absurdly short warranty period as an obvious tell
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly specific absurd detail. Two hours is such a ridiculous warranty period that it becomes the perfect tell for a fake.

jokes.text: And you should have known that the Jackson on a 20 ain't Jermaine.
jokes.setup: Continuing to mediate the fake money/fake watch dispute
jokes.punchline: Pointing out the obvious currency knowledge about which Jackson is on the twenty
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay on the Jackson family name versus Andrew Jackson on currency. Very quotable line.

jokes.text: They all look alike to me.
jokes.setup: Discussion about distinguishing between different Jacksons
jokes.punchline: Tyriq (presumably Black) using the racist 'they all look alike' line about other Black people
jokes.explanation: Subversive twist on racist stereotype by having a Black character use it ironically. Risky but contextually works.

jokes.text: He's the source who revealed Color Me Badd was white.
jokes.setup: Second reference to Willy's insider knowledge
jokes.punchline: Another 'revelation' about racial ambiguity in 90s pop culture
jokes.explanation: Decent callback but less impactful than the Mariah Carey version. Color Me Badd was more obviously white.

jokes.text: Speaking hypodermically.
jokes.setup: Will saying 'hypothetically' about choosing a partner
jokes.punchline: Tyriq's malapropism mixing 'hypothetically' with 'hypodermically'
jokes.explanation: Good character work showing Tyriq's limited vocabulary. The medical term substitution is appropriately absurd.

jokes.text: Yo, Mr. Banks, my tubby judicial brother.
jokes.setup: Jazz addressing Phil respectfully
jokes.punchline: Adding 'tubby' as a casual descriptor in what's meant to be a respectful greeting
jokes.explanation: Classic Jazz moment - trying to be respectful but unable to resist the insult. The casual delivery sells it.

jokes.text: Would you mind standing downwind?
jokes.setup: Geoffrey noting Will obviously didn't shower
jokes.punchline: Asks Will to move so his smell doesn't affect Geoffrey
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of smell joke but fairly predictable

jokes.text: I guess I got a, kind of, Pepé Le Pew thing going here.
jokes.setup: Geoffrey telling Will to stand downwind
jokes.punchline: Will comparing his body odor situation to the notoriously smelly cartoon skunk
jokes.explanation: Decent self-aware reference. Pepé Le Pew is the perfect comparison for someone with serious body odor issues.

jokes.text: At this rate? About the time Queen Elizabeth opens for Queen Latifah.
jokes.setup: Being asked when coffee will be ready given the water situation
jokes.punchline: Suggesting an impossibly long timeframe by imagining British royalty opening for a rapper
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Geoffrey line combining his British sensibility with hip-hop culture knowledge. The absurd image of Queen Elizabeth as an opening act is perfect.

jokes.text: Man, you sure can't. You should probably call in ugly.
jokes.setup: Hilary saying she can't go to work looking unwashed
jokes.punchline: Will suggesting she call in 'ugly' instead of sick
jokes.explanation: Good wordplay on 'calling in sick.' The timing and delivery would make this effective, though not groundbreaking.

jokes.text: the Moms Mabley look is definitely passé.
jokes.setup: Will criticizing Hilary's appearance
jokes.punchline: Carlton's overly sophisticated reference to the elderly Black comedian's appearance
jokes.explanation: Very Carlton - his pretentious vocabulary applied to roasting. Moms Mabley reference shows his unexpected cultural knowledge.

jokes.text: I'm sorry, but I had to wash. I have enough problems. I'm going through puberty for God's sake.
jokes.setup: Ashley defending her long shower
jokes.punchline: Young girl treating puberty as additional justification for hygiene needs
jokes.explanation: Age-appropriate character humor. Ashley's dramatic justification for basic hygiene is relatable and mildly amusing.

jokes.text: Hey, Aunt Viv, I'm telling you, if we band together we can take him.
jokes.setup: Phil presumably doing something that prompts a group intervention
jokes.punchline: Will suggesting they physically overpower Phil as a family unit
jokes.explanation: Decent physical comedy setup. The idea of needing to gang up on Phil suggests he's either very large or very determined.

jokes.text: Leave him alone, honey, he's mine.
jokes.setup: Will suggesting they gang up on Uncle Phil
jokes.punchline: Aunt Viv claiming exclusive rights to deal with Phil
jokes.explanation: Good marital humor showing Vivian's territorial claim over punishing Phil

jokes.text: if Mr. Goodwrench wants to visit Mrs. Toolbox... he'd better get Mr. Water running.
jokes.setup: Vivian addressing Phil's desire for intimacy versus his repair failures
jokes.punchline: Sexual metaphor using tool/repair terminology
jokes.explanation: Clever sexual innuendo that ties into the repair theme. Vivian's sophisticated delivery makes it funnier.

jokes.text: a show both salubrious and sudorific.
jokes.setup: Jazz trying to impress Will with his vocabulary
jokes.punchline: Using unnecessarily complex words that probably don't mean what he thinks they mean
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Jazz trying to sound smart. The words are real but inappropriately used, which is the joke.

jokes.text: You got a little salube on the side of your lip, too.
jokes.setup: Jazz using the word 'salubrious'
jokes.punchline: Will mocking Jazz by pretending 'salube' is a physical substance
jokes.explanation: Clever way of deflating Jazz's pretentious vocabulary by treating the fancy word as something gross and visible.

jokes.text: Nice weather we're having. Guess that's 'cause we're the third planet from the sun. Unlike Mercury, where the surface is perpetually molten.
jokes.setup: Normal small talk opening
jokes.punchline: Tyriq immediately launching into random astronomy facts to seem smart
jokes.explanation: Good character work showing Tyriq's attempt to appear intellectual. The transition from weather to planetary science is absurd.

jokes.text: Look, a little nasty piece of carpet with a vest.
jokes.setup: Someone (Winky) entering the scene
jokes.punchline: Will describing Winky as basically a moving carpet
jokes.explanation: Vivid visual insult. The specificity of 'piece of carpet with a vest' creates a clear mental image.

jokes.text: Man, Winky was my father's name.
jokes.setup: Will asking where he got the stupid name Winky
jokes.punchline: Winky's matter-of-fact explanation that it's a family name
jokes.explanation: Good deadpan response. The idea of 'Winky' being passed down through generations is inherently funny.

jokes.text: Yeah, what Will needs is intellectual capacitance.
jokes.setup: Arguing about who should be Will's partner
jokes.punchline: Jazz using 'capacitance' (electrical term) instead of 'capacity'
jokes.explanation: Another Jazz vocabulary mishap. 'Capacitance' is a real word but wrong context, showing his pseudo-intellectual tendencies.

jokes.text: It appears he's been getting notes warning him to get out of town.
jokes.setup: Leiberbaum suddenly leaving for airport
jokes.punchline: Revealing the game show competition has escalated to mob-style intimidation
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation of the stakes. Treating a game show like organized crime is perfectly absurd.

jokes.text: And to go on this game show was his last desperate wish... before he takes that old dirt nap.
jokes.setup: Will lying about Carlton having a rare disease
jokes.punchline: Euphemistically describing death as Carlton's 'old dirt nap'
jokes.explanation: Good dark humor with casual euphemism for death. Will's commitment to the lie makes it funnier.

jokes.text: He got Bette Davis eyes.
jokes.setup: Being pressed about Carlton's supposed disease
jokes.punchline: Will citing the Kim Carnes song title as a medical condition
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist moment. Using a pop song as a fake disease name is inspired, and the delivery would be perfect.

jokes.text: Will, can I borrow your Walkman? I'm going out for a five-mile run.
jokes.setup: Will just claimed Carlton has fatal disease
jokes.punchline: Carlton casually asks to borrow Walkman for exercise
jokes.explanation: Perfect timing showing Carlton's complete health right after Will's lie about dying

jokes.text: and let me choose my words carefully... you guys are stupid.
jokes.setup: Carlton promising to choose his words carefully
jokes.punchline: The 'careful' word choice is just bluntly calling them stupid
jokes.explanation: Perfect Carlton moment - his idea of careful diplomacy is still brutally direct. The misdirection works well.

jokes.text: Letters not on a phone dial? Q and Z. Best picture, 1936? The Great Ziegfeld. The third wife of Julius Caesar? Calpurnia.
jokes.setup: Will demonstrating Carlton's knowledge with trivia questions
jokes.punchline: Carlton's increasingly obscure and specific knowledge culminating in ancient Roman history
jokes.explanation: Great character showcase. The progression from practical knowledge to ancient history shows Carlton's academic nature.

jokes.text: And by the way, can you remind me when to breathe?
jokes.setup: Jazz sarcastically playing up stupidity
jokes.punchline: Tyriq escalates the sarcasm further
jokes.explanation: Decent escalation but fairly predictable sarcastic exaggeration

jokes.text: The wind in my face will dry my tears.
jokes.setup: Will realizing he sacrificed his friendships for a car
jokes.punchline: Overly dramatic rationalization that at least the convertible will help with crying
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Will's dramatic tendencies. The logic is absurdly practical yet emotional.

jokes.text: Yeah, or a real tight butt.
jokes.setup: Carlton saying Clarence Thomas looks for dignity in clerks
jokes.punchline: Will referencing the Anita Hill sexual harassment allegations
jokes.explanation: Sharp topical humor referencing the then-recent Thomas confirmation hearings. Edgy but contextually appropriate.

jokes.text: when you got home around 10:00 and... your drawers ain't get here till the next morning.
jokes.setup: Will needing Carlton's help and threatening to reveal embarrassing information
jokes.punchline: Implying Carlton had some kind of underwear-related incident/adventure
jokes.explanation: Good blackmail humor suggesting Carlton had some kind of wild night. The underwear detail makes it funnier.

jokes.text: Dr. Jazzy Jeff... M.D., Ph.D., and O.P.P.
jokes.setup: Introducing Jazz as a brain surgeon
jokes.punchline: Adding the Naughty by Nature song reference to his credentials
jokes.explanation: Clever mixing of academic credentials with hip-hop references. The O.P.P. addition is perfectly absurd.

jokes.text: And how long has he been dating Whitney Houston?
jokes.setup: Learning Jazz supposedly dates Whitney Houston
jokes.punchline: Will's incredulous reaction focusing on the relationship timeline
jokes.explanation: Decent reaction humor. Will's focus on 'how long' suggests jealousy over the celebrity relationship.

jokes.text: The beloved pachyderm in question won our young hearts by turning a handicap into--
jokes.setup: Simple question about Dumbo the elephant
jokes.punchline: Carlton's unnecessarily verbose and academic response to obvious question
jokes.explanation: Perfect Carlton character moment - overcomplicating a simple answer. His verbosity costs them points.

jokes.text: I'm sorry. Time is up. Let's go to your opponents.
jokes.setup: Carlton giving overly elaborate answer about Dumbo
jokes.punchline: Host cuts off Carlton's verbose response
jokes.explanation: Perfect Carlton moment showing his overthinking simple questions on game shows

jokes.text: That would be the capital 'O,' Bob.
jokes.setup: Asked 'What is the capital of Ohio?'
jokes.punchline: Tyriq answers with literal capital letter instead of city
jokes.explanation: Brilliant wordplay that subverts expectations - technically correct but wrong, perfectly delivered

jokes.text: That would be the capital 'O,' Bob.
jokes.setup: Question about Ohio's capital city
jokes.punchline: Tyriq interpreting 'capital' as the letter rather than the city
jokes.explanation: Clever misunderstanding that's both wrong and technically logical. Shows Tyriq's literal-minded thinking.

jokes.text: Sounds like the judges drank their lunch.
jokes.setup: Judges accepting Tyriq's obviously wrong answer
jokes.punchline: Will suggesting the judges are intoxicated to explain their poor judgment
jokes.explanation: Good observational humor about game show inconsistency. 'Drank their lunch' is a nice euphemism.

jokes.text: 'In the plains,' Bob!
jokes.setup: Carlton giving another overly academic answer to simple question
jokes.punchline: Will cutting him off with the simple, correct answer
jokes.explanation: Good teamwork humor. Will's exasperation with Carlton's verbose style creates the comedy.

jokes.text: 'In the plains,' Bob!
jokes.setup: Carlton starts elaborate answer about Spanish rainfall
jokes.punchline: Will cuts him off with simple correct answer
jokes.explanation: Good contrast between Carlton's overthinking and Will's practical approach

jokes.text: Wait. That joint ain't gonna fill up with water or nothing, Bob, is it?
jokes.setup: Being asked to enter the soundproof booth
jokes.punchline: Will's suspicion that the game show might be more dangerous than advertised
jokes.explanation: Good game show parody paranoia. References the dangerous stunts while showing Will's street-smart wariness.

jokes.text: I'm sorry, Bob, this is a bad time.
jokes.setup: Being asked to answer the friendship question on live TV
jokes.punchline: Tyriq treating the game show like an inconvenient phone call
jokes.explanation: Brilliant non-sequitur treating live television like a personal phone call. Perfectly captures Tyriq's character.

tmdb_overview: Needing a game show partner, Will must choose between bickering friends.
